methods.method_name: Electroless Gold Deposition on Diatom Frustules
methods.description: This method describes the process of coating diatom frustules with gold using an electroless deposition technique. The purpose is to enhance the surface properties of the frustules for further analysis.

steps.description: Diatom frustules were first incubated with hydrogen peroxide solution (35 %) over 24 h to increase the density of oxygen groups on the surface.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate diatom frustules with hydrogen peroxide to increase surface oxygen groups

steps.description: Sensitization of frustules in a solution of 0.026 M SnCl2 and 0.07 M trifluoroacetic acid for 45 min followed by rinsing in Milli-Q water.
steps.searchable_summary: sensitize frustules with SnCl2 and trifluoroacetic acid solution

steps.description: Activation step, where the sensitized frustules were immersed into a 100-mL ammoniacal solution of 0.029 M AgNO3 for 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: activate frustules with ammoniacal AgNO3 solution

steps.description: Plating/galvanic displacement step: the activated frustules were immersed into a standard prebath solution for 1 h followed by a plating bath for the required deposition time (16-88 h).
steps.searchable_summary: perform galvanic displacement with Au plating solution

methods.description: This method involves the examination of gold-coated frustules using scanning electron microscopy and the collection of Raman spectra to analyze surface properties.

steps.description: Gold-coated frustules were examined by scanning electron microscopy (JEOL JSM-6400) equipped by energy dispersive X-ray analysis (EDAX) spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: examine gold-coated frustules using SEM and EDAX

steps.description: The Raman spectra of pMA (both spontaneous and surface enhanced) were collected by a confocal Raman spectrometer operating with a diode laser excitation source emitting at 785 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Raman spectra using confocal Raman spectrometer

steps.description: The Raman band of a silicon wafer at 520 cm−1 was used to calibrate the spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate Raman spectrometer using silicon wafer
